mappings.rationaleAI: Core text on the art of architecture covering design elements, building types, and architectural theory (A.1 through A.5).
Covers modern architectural movements including reinforced concrete and key architects like Wright, Mies van der Rohe, and Le Corbusier (A.7.a).
Covers British domestic and public architecture including the architectural profession, relevant to Western architectural history (A.5, A.7.a).
Directly covers garden and landscape design including Olmsted, urban design, and landscape gardening (B).
Covers the history of garden design from ancient to modern periods (B).
Directly addresses urban design as a discipline, the third major topic of this section alongside architecture and landscape design.
Covers constructional systems such as arches, vaults, buttresses, and concrete relevant to structural elements of buildings (A.2.b, A.3).
Covers properties of building materials including cement, ceramics, sand, and stone explicitly listed as architectural materials (A.2.a).
Provides philosophical context for aesthetic aspects of architecture including beauty and design judgment (A.1, A.4).
Covers Egyptian temples and tombs as examples of religious and commemorative architecture (A.5.b) and non-Western architectural traditions (A.7.b).
Byzantine architecture including Hagia Sophia exemplifies domed construction and religious architecture (A.2.b, A.5.b, A.7.a).
Islamic architectural traditions including mosques are explicitly listed under religious architecture (A.5.b) and represent a major non-Western tradition (A.7.b).
Covers suburban residential planning and urbanism, relevant to domestic architecture and urban design (A.5.a).
Addresses sustainable design practices relevant to contemporary architecture and urban planning, including energy flows and environmental impact.
